perturbation.original_text: 1.5 Right of Recovery. The Investment Adviser shall have the option to recover the full amount waived, paid or reimbursed (the Expense Waiver) by the Investment Adviser on any day on which the Fund's Net Investment Income exceeds zero. In no event, however, shall the Expense Waiver, or any portion thereof, constitute an obligation of the Fund to the Investment Adviser on any such day unless the Investment Adviser has expressly exercised its right to recover all or a portion of the Expense Waiver on that day, in which case such portion of the Expense Waiver elected by the Investment Adviser for recovery that day shall then be due and payable. If, and to the extent, the payment by the Fund to the Investment Adviser of the Expense Waiver would cause the Fund's Net Investment Income to fall below zero, such payment shall be deferred until such date on which the
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 Right of Recovery. The Investment Adviser shall have the obligation to recover the full amount waived, paid or reimbursed (the Expense Waiver) by the Investment Adviser on any day on which the Fund's Net Investment Income exceeds zero. In no event, however, shall the Expense Waiver, or any portion thereof, constitute an obligation of the Fund to the Investment Adviser on any such day unless the Investment Adviser has expressly exercised its right to recover all or a portion of the Expense Waiver on that day, in which case such portion of the Expense Waiver elected by the Investment Adviser for recovery that day shall then be due and payable. If, and to the extent, the payment by the Fund to the Investment Adviser of the Expense Waiver would cause the Fund's Net Investment Income to fall below zero, such payment shall be deferred until such date on which the
perturbation.explanation: Changing "option" to "obligation" in Section 1.5 contradicts the rest of the section. The original language specifies the Investment Adviser *has the option* to recover waived expenses. By changing it to *the obligation* to recover the fund is now required to recover those waivered expenses even if it does not want to. This creates an in-text contradiction because later in the same section it states, "In no event, however, shall the Expense Waiver, or any portion thereof, constitute an obligation of the Fund to the Investment Adviser on any such day unless the Investment Adviser has expressly exercised its right to recover all or a portion of the Expense Waiver on that day". Now it is contradicting itself that the Investment Advisor has the obligation, while still saying the Fund is not obliged to pay for the waivered expenses unless the Investment Adviser wants the fund to pay it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.5 Right of Recovery. The Investment Adviser shall have the obligation to recover the full amount waived, paid or reimbursed (the Expense Waiver) by the Investment Adviser on any day on which the Fund's Net Investment Income exceeds zero. In no event, however, shall the Expense Waiver, or any portion thereof, constitute an obligation of the Fund to the Investment Adviser on any such day unless the Investment Adviser has expressly exercised its right to recover all or a portion of the Expense Waiver on that day, in which case such portion of the Expense Waiver elected by the Investment Adviser for recovery that day shall then be due and payable. If, and to the extent, the payment by the Fund to the Investment Adviser of the Expense Waiver would cause the Fund's Net Investment Income to fall below zero, such payment shall be deferred until such date on which the
perturbation.justification: The change from "option" to "obligation" creates a direct conflict within Section 1.5 itself. Initially, the Investment Adviser has a *choice* regarding expense recovery. Post-change, they *must* recover expenses when Net Investment Income is positive. However, the subsequent sentence states the Fund is *not* obligated to pay unless the Investment Adviser exercises its right. The contradictory nature is that the Investment Adviser is now obligated to recover, but the Fund is not obligated to pay unless the Investment Adviser chooses to recover. The contradictory language within the changed text makes these clauses incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall continue in effect for a period of one year from the date of its execution and from year to year thereafter provided such continuance is specifically approved by a majority of the trustees of the Trust who (i) are not "interested persons" of the Trust or any other party to this Agreement, as defined in the 1940 Act, and (ii) have no direct or indirect financial interest in the operation of this Agreement ("Non- Interested Trustees"). This Agreement will terminate upon the termination of the Advisory Agreement except the Investment Adviser's Right of Recovery set forth in section 1.5 herein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall continue in effect indefinitely from the date of its execution. This Agreement will terminate upon the termination of the Advisory Agreement except the Investment Adviser's Right of Recovery set forth in section 1.5 herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the Term and Termination of Agreement creates an in-text contradiction because the original text specifies the contract auto-renews on a year to year basis and approval needs to be voted on by the trustees who are not interested persons, while the change completely removes the renewal condition. Now the contract lasts indefinitely and goes against the original text. The agreement continuing in effect indefinitely now is in direct contrast to the requirement for annual approval by the Non-Interested Trustees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall continue in effect indefinitely from the date of its execution. This Agreement will terminate upon the termination of the Advisory Agreement except the Investment Adviser's Right of Recovery set forth in section 1.5 herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for annual renewal and approval by Non-Interested Trustees, stating the agreement continues indefinitely. This directly contradicts the original text's provision for annual review and approval, creating a structural flaw and leading to uncertainty about the agreement's term and enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: If the Investment Adviser is required to reimburse the Fund, the Investment Adviser shall make such payment within 30 days after each month-end in the amount due the Fund as of each month. If the Fund is required to pay the Investment Adviser the Expense Waiver, the Fund shall make such payment within 30 days after each month-end. Reimbursements to the Fund or the Expense Waiver shall not include any additional charges or fees whatsoever, including, e.g., interest accruable on such reimbursements or the Expense Waiver.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Investment Adviser is required to reimburse the Fund, the Investment Adviser shall make such payment within 30 days after each month-end in the amount due the Fund as of each month. If the Fund is required to pay the Investment Adviser the Expense Waiver, the Fund shall make such payment within 30 days after each month-end, unless otherwise decided by the Fund's board of directors. Reimbursements to the Fund or the Expense Waiver shall not include any additional charges or fees whatsoever, including, e.g., interest accruable on such reimbursements or the Expense Waiver.
perturbation.explanation: Adding "unless otherwise decided by the Fund's board of directors" after stating the Fund is required to pay the Investment Advisor within 30 days after each month-end creates an in-text contradiction. The original text clearly states that the Fund is required to make the payment within 30 days, the change allows the Fund's board of directors to decide otherwise, giving them the option to delay, modify, or reject the payments. Now it's contradictory because it allows the Fund's Board of Directors to deviate from the explicit 30-day timeframe, undermining the initial commitment to timely reimbursement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the Investment Adviser is required to reimburse the Fund, the Investment Adviser shall make such payment within 30 days after each month-end in the amount due the Fund as of each month. If the Fund is required to pay the Investment Adviser the Expense Waiver, the Fund shall make such payment within 30 days after each month-end, unless otherwise decided by the Fund's board of directors. Reimbursements to the Fund or the Expense Waiver shall not include any additional charges or fees whatsoever, including, e.g., interest accruable on such reimbursements or the Expense Waiver.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates payment within 30 days after each month-end. The changed text introduces an exception, 'unless otherwise decided by the Fund's board of directors,' allowing the board to deviate from this timeframe. This creates a direct contradiction, as the initial mandatory payment timeframe is no longer absolute, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes over the timing of payments.
